$comment: This schema validates an individual recipe entry object. The registry YAML file (recipe-registry.yaml) has a 'recipes' array at the top level; each element in that array must conform to this schema. Applying this schema to the full registry file will fail because the top-level object has a 'recipes' key rather than the 'name', 'language', etc. fields defined here. To validate the full file, use a wrapper schema that defines the top-level structure with 'recipes' as an array of items referencing this entry schema.
title: Recipe Registry Entry
description: JSON Schema Draft 7 for individual recipe entries in recipe-registry.yaml. Each element of the top-level 'recipes' array must conform to this schema.

language.description: Target programming language (e.g., python, typescript, ruby)

engine.description: Refactoring engine used by this recipe (e.g., rope, ts-morph, synvert, ast)

adapter.description: Path to adapter script relative to plugins/dso/scripts/recipe-adapters/ (e.g., rope-adapter.sh)

capability_description.description: Human-readable description of what this recipe does, with explicit scope

scope.description: Scope of the recipe's effect (e.g., single-file, multi-file, cross-file, cross-package, function, project)

min_engine_version.description: Minimum required version of the engine (semver string). Passed to adapters as the RECIPE_MIN_ENGINE_VERSION environment variable (see recipe-engine-adapter contract).

installation_instructions.description: Instructions for installing the required engine (e.g., pip install rope>=1.7.0)

framework.description: Target framework (e.g., flask, nextjs, django, react)

recipe_type.description: transform modifies existing files; generative creates new files

$comment: The 'not' constraint below prohibits the 'cli_command_template' property entirely using JSON Schema Draft 7 semantics (not+required means invalid when the named property is present). This field is banned because recipe parameters MUST be passed via RECIPE_PARAM_* env vars, not CLI arguments, to avoid shell interpolation hazards. Removing or relaxing this constraint would break the env-var-only parameter protocol defined in the recipe engine adapter contract.
